Foaming tides flow forever, frothing freezing fjords. Fierce fish flee fast, flipping fins find far-off flats. Frequent fog follows, forming fragile frontiers, forever formidable frontiers.

Write one paragraph about the ocean in which every word begins with the letter F.
fixed

nodes.title: Model & hardware

properties.text: On first run the Loader auto-downloads google/diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it (~53 GB, bf16) from HuggingFace and caches it. The model card wants >=60 GB GPU for a full bf16 load; smaller cards offload to CPU and run slower. quant=none is the only honest setting (the fused MoE experts can't be bitsandbytes-quantized). Turn local_files_only ON once it's cached to run offline.

widgets_values: On first run the Loader auto-downloads google/diffusiongemma-26B-A4B-it (~53 GB, bf16) from HuggingFace and caches it. The model card wants >=60 GB GPU for a full bf16 load; smaller cards offload to CPU and run slower. quant=none is the only honest setting (the fused MoE experts can't be bitsandbytes-quantized). Turn local_files_only ON once it's cached to run offline.

nodes.title: Knobs to play with

properties.text: num_inference_steps: how many denoise passes. t_min/t_max: temperature window — a hotter, longer schedule delays premature commitment. entropy_bound + confidence: the per-step rule for which tokens freeze. gen_length: canvas size. thinking: toggle the model's reasoning channel. Good first-run values: steps 48, t 0.4-0.8, entropy_bound 0.05, confidence 0.005, gen_length 1024.

widgets_values: num_inference_steps: how many denoise passes. t_min/t_max: temperature window — a hotter, longer schedule delays premature commitment. entropy_bound + confidence: the per-step rule for which tokens freeze. gen_length: canvas size. thinking: toggle the model's reasoning channel. Good first-run values: steps 48, t 0.4-0.8, entropy_bound 0.05, confidence 0.005, gen_length 1024.

nodes.title: Outputs & what to watch

properties.text: Two per-step flipbooks: `frames` (text, a STRING list -> Preview Text) and `images` (rendered frames, an IMAGE list -> Preview Image, shareable). `canvas_trace` -> DGemmaTrace draws a commit heatmap + summary. Watch the commit-front spread: a fluent-but-wrong answer reveals itself as an early, confident lock-in. (The `images` output requires a build that includes it — issue #21.)

widgets_values: Two per-step flipbooks: `frames` (text, a STRING list -> Preview Text) and `images` (rendered frames, an IMAGE list -> Preview Image, shareable). `canvas_trace` -> DGemmaTrace draws a commit heatmap + summary. Watch the commit-front spread: a fluent-but-wrong answer reveals itself as an early, confident lock-in. (The `images` output requires a build that includes it — issue #21.)

nodes.title: What this is

properties.text: DiffusionGemma writes text by DIFFUSION: it denoises a whole canvas of tokens over N steps instead of left-to-right. This graph instruments that process so you can watch the answer crystallize step by step. The draw here is observability, not speed.

widgets_values: DiffusionGemma writes text by DIFFUSION: it denoises a whole canvas of tokens over N steps instead of left-to-right. This graph instruments that process so you can watch the answer crystallize step by step. The draw here is observability, not speed.

nodes.title: Heatmap — watch the answer crystallize

properties.text: Each pixel = one token position at one step. HORIZONTAL = canvas positions (left = start of the generated region, right = end). VERTICAL = denoising steps (top = first, bottom = last). WHITE = that position CHANGED this step (still being renoised, not settled). BLACK = it held steady (locked in). Read top-to-bottom to watch the answer freeze: the top row is all white (nothing committed yet), and black spreads down as positions stop changing. The white->black boundary is the COMMIT FRONT. A front that snaps to black early and all at once = premature crystallization (committed before it finished thinking - the fluent-but-wrong trap); a front that stays white longer settled gradually. NOTE: white = still moving, black = locked - it's a 'what's still changing' map, not a 'what's filled in' map.

widgets_values: Each pixel = one token position at one step. HORIZONTAL = canvas positions (left = start of the generated region, right = end). VERTICAL = denoising steps (top = first, bottom = last). WHITE = that position CHANGED this step (still being renoised, not settled). BLACK = it held steady (locked in). Read top-to-bottom to watch the answer freeze: the top row is all white (nothing committed yet), and black spreads down as positions stop changing. The white->black boundary is the COMMIT FRONT. A front that snaps to black early and all at once = premature crystallization (committed before it finished thinking - the fluent-but-wrong trap); a front that stays white longer settled gradually. NOTE: white = still moving, black = locked - it's a 'what's still changing' map, not a 'what's filled in' map.

nodes.title: CanvasState — is the answer actually done?

properties.text: The honesty readout beside the text. A bare string can't say whether its canvas finished denoising - too few steps or too wide an entropy bound leaves finished-LOOKING text that's still uncommitted noise. converged: True only if the last step accepted every position under the entropy bound (the schedule bottomed out) - NOT a claim EOS fired or that tokens stayed locked. committed_fraction: fraction of positions locked on the final step. steps_used: steps actually run (early-stop can finish before the max). turn_closed: True only if a real end-of-turn token was committed in the answer; False = the turn never truly stopped (empty/all-thought answer, or it hit the length budget mid-thought). thought / stray_thought_delimiter: the excised reasoning channel + a malformed-leftover flag. THE TELL: converged=True but turn_closed=False -> the text looks done but didn't really finish.

widgets_values: The honesty readout beside the text. A bare string can't say whether its canvas finished denoising - too few steps or too wide an entropy bound leaves finished-LOOKING text that's still uncommitted noise. converged: True only if the last step accepted every position under the entropy bound (the schedule bottomed out) - NOT a claim EOS fired or that tokens stayed locked. committed_fraction: fraction of positions locked on the final step. steps_used: steps actually run (early-stop can finish before the max). turn_closed: True only if a real end-of-turn token was committed in the answer; False = the turn never truly stopped (empty/all-thought answer, or it hit the length budget mid-thought). thought / stray_thought_delimiter: the excised reasoning channel + a malformed-leftover flag. THE TELL: converged=True but turn_closed=False -> the text looks done but didn't really finish.

nodes.title: Trace Summary — the run's vitals

properties.text: A text digest of the whole run. scheduler + config: which scheduler drove it (default EntropyBoundScheduler) and its knobs (entropy_bound, t_min/t_max, num_inference_steps). steps: denoising steps actually captured (fewer than the max if it early-stopped). committed_fraction per step: the AVALANCHE CURVE - fraction of positions locked in at each step, climbing toward 1.0. Watch how fast it climbs; a steep early jump means the model committed hard and fast (and may have locked a wrong basin before it finished thinking). mask-token corroboration: confirms this model does pure uniform-state renoise with no fixed 'MASK' sentinel; it flags a candidate if one ever appears.

widgets_values: A text digest of the whole run. scheduler + config: which scheduler drove it (default EntropyBoundScheduler) and its knobs (entropy_bound, t_min/t_max, num_inference_steps). steps: denoising steps actually captured (fewer than the max if it early-stopped). committed_fraction per step: the AVALANCHE CURVE - fraction of positions locked in at each step, climbing toward 1.0. Watch how fast it climbs; a steep early jump means the model committed hard and fast (and may have locked a wrong basin before it finished thinking). mask-token corroboration: confirms this model does pure uniform-state renoise with no fixed 'MASK' sentinel; it flags a candidate if one ever appears.
